overview: Izzet Spells is a tempo strategy that leverages cheap, synergistic instants and sorceries to establish early board presence while generating card advantage. By cycling through the deck rapidly, the pilot aims to outpace opponents with consistent value generation and disruptive interaction. The deck relies on spell-triggered effects to maintain pressure and control the pace of the game.
win_conditions: Casting a high volume of spells to trigger draw effects
Developing a resilient board presence with spell-activated creatures
Overwhelming opponents with cumulative value and direct damage

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more cheap spells
Hands containing a signature card paired with interaction
Hands that can play a turn-one spell or creature
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or card draw
Hands missing land drops for the first three turns

early_game: Play cheap spells to trigger Stormchaser's Talent and establish early tempo
Deploy Slickshot Show-Off to apply pressure and enable spell synergies
Hold interaction for opponent's early threats
mid_game: Cast Flow State to accelerate card advantage and stabilize the board
Use removal and counterspells to protect key permanents
Cycle through the deck to find finishers and additional value engines
late_game: Leverage accumulated card advantage to out-resource opponents
Maintain board control while chipping away at life totals
Commit to all-in spells when the opponent runs out of answers

key_cards.name: Stormchaser's Talent
key_cards.role: Generates card advantage when spells are cast
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting it early to fuel the deck's spell synergy and maintain hand size

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.role: Provides early board presence and spell synergy
key_cards.notes: Use as a turn-one or turn-two play to establish tempo and trigger spell-based effects

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.role: Rewards casting multiple spells with value or draw
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have a sequence of spells ready to maximize its trigger potential and stabilize the game

sequencing_tips: Hold up interaction until the opponent commits to a threat
Cast cheap spells before playing permanents to maximize trigger counts
Prioritize drawing cards over dealing damage when behind on tempo
Save removal for high-impact threats rather than early creatures

matchups.advice: Focus on early disruption and card draw to outlast the initial rush
Apply consistent pressure and use spell synergy to generate value faster than their answers
Race them with efficient spells while protecting your key permanents
